chunk_summaries: The group brainstormed research questions for modeling pathogen movement at ecological and molecular scales, using a shared document to guide the discussion. The tone was collaborative and productive, with participants actively building on each other's ideas. The group ended the chunk in a divergent exploration phase, having identified several potential research directions but no specific project.

The group discussed integrating temporal, spatial, and imaging data into pathogen models, with Tavis Anderson and Laurene Tetard proposing specific technical contributions. The tone was collaborative and focused on finding common ground, with participants showing consistent engagement through nods and backchannels. The group ended the chunk in an ambiguous state, having identified a shared interest in data integration but lacking a finalized project plan.

The group continued to explore the integration of high-resolution imaging and surveillance data for modeling pathogen movement. Tavis Anderson and Rebecca Garabed drove the discussion, with other participants contributing domain-specific insights. The tone remained collaborative and positive, with a notable moment of shared humor, and the group ended the chunk with a shared interest in the proposed approach but still lacking a concrete project plan.

The group focused on the technical integration of Bayesian machine learning and imaging data, with Laurene Tetard and Tavis Anderson leading the discussion. The tone was collaborative and focused, with participants beginning to use more joint language to describe their potential work. The group ended the chunk with a shared interest in exploring literature to support their integration approach, though a specific project plan remains to be defined.

The group converged on a shared research framework, focusing on the integration of surveillance data and predictive modeling to improve resource allocation. Participants actively synthesized their disciplinary perspectives, with Silvie Huijben and Kezia Manlove driving the discussion toward more specific, actionable research goals. The group ended the chunk with a clearer, more focused research direction and a commitment to further refining their methodological approach.
